steps.title: Expand pv by substituting and using FOIL
steps.content:
Replace $p = 3x + 4$ and $v = x + 5$ into $pv$:
$$pv = (3x + 4)(x + 5)$$
Expand using FOIL:
$$= 3x \cdot x + 3x \cdot 5 + 4 \cdot x + 4 \cdot 5$$
$$= 3x^2 + 15x + 4x + 20$$
$$= 3x^2 + 19x + 20$$

steps.title: Compute -2p and gather all components
steps.content:
Expand $-2p$ where $p = 3x + 4$:
$$-2p = -2(3x + 4) = -6x - 8$$
We already have $v = x + 5$. Now combine all three pieces:
$$pv - 2p + v = (3x^2 + 19x + 20) + (-6x - 8) + (x + 5)$$

steps.title: Combine like terms to find the final expression
steps.content:
Collect the $x^2$, $x$, and constant terms:
$$= 3x^2 + (19 - 6 + 1)x + (20 - 8 + 5)$$
$$= 3x^2 + 14x + 17$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
